author: Sofia Martínez
title: How Dance Studios Use Marketing Automation to Fill Classes
description: Automate trial class bookings, retain students, and reduce admin time with email workflows. We show you the exact setup dance studio owners use.

sections.p: We work with 12+ dance studios, and they all share the same problem: managing class schedules, trial bookings, and student retention takes 8-10 hours per week. Most are still using paper sign-ups and forwarding emails between instructors. Marketing automation fixes this. We're talking about automated trial-to-paid workflows that convert 28-35% of free class attendees into paying members—versus 12% without automation.

sections.h2: The Trial Class Funnel That Works

sections.p: A trial class is your biggest conversion lever. Here's the flow we build for studios: someone submits a web form or clicks a link from Instagram, they get an immediate confirmation email with logistics, a reminder 24 hours before class, and a post-class survey asking why they'd want to join. If they don't book within 7 days, a nurture sequence starts—3 emails over 14 days highlighting different class styles, success stories, and a limited-time discount (usually 25% off first month).

One studio in Chicago automated this using HubSpot free tier. They went from 18 trial classes per month to 34 in 3 months. The automation handled the reminders and follow-ups, so their front desk staff spent zero extra time on it.

list: Confirmation email with calendar link + studio location/parking info
24-hour SMS reminder (60% open rate vs. email's 22%)
Post-class survey with Net Promoter Score question
7-day re-engagement email if no booking yet

sections.p: New members churn hardest in weeks 2-6. We set up automation that triggers based on class attendance: if someone attends fewer than 2 classes in a 14-day window, they get a check-in email from the studio director (personalized, not generic). If they've attended 4+ classes, they get invited to a private workshop or community event. This keeps showing up in their inbox as value, not as a gym nagging them.

sections.quote: Our churn rate dropped from 34% to 19% in 6 months just by sending the right message at the right time. Automation meant we could do this without hiring someone.

sections.p: The technical setup is simple: link your class attendance system (or manually tag attendance in your CRM) to trigger conditional emails. Platforms like Zapier can connect your booking app to Mailchimp or ConvertKit in under 20 minutes.

Most studios offer multiple class types: ballet, hip-hop, contemporary, kids. Automation lets you cross-sell. If a student takes only hip-hop, tag them in your CRM. After their 8th class, send a targeted email featuring your contemporary night and invite them to a free drop-in. This works because you're personalizing based on real behavior, not guessing.

list: Segment by age group and skill level (beginner, intermediate, advanced)
Trigger cross-sell emails after 8 classes in primary style
Create Instagram custom audiences from email list to retarget non-engaged members
Send monthly class schedule + instructor spotlight emails (4 emails/month minimum)
Birthday month special offer (25% off any package)

sections.p: For studios under 200 members: Mailchimp (free tier works) + Zapier (14/month) to connect your booking system. For 200-500 members: HubSpot free CRM + Mailchimp paid ($20/month). For 500+: Klaviyo ($25-100/month) because it has better segmentation for repeat bookings.
The key isn't the tool—it's the workflow. We've seen studios using a $5/month email service outperform studios on Klaviyo because they had a real strategy and actually used automation consistently.
